Business.title: Business
Business.description: Represents a business using the platform to manage their online presence.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Business entity.

address.description: The physical address of the business.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the business.

websiteUrl.description: The website URL of the business.

GoogleMyBusinessProfile.title: GoogleMyBusinessProfile
GoogleMyBusinessProfile.description: Represents a Google My Business profile associated with a business.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GoogleMyBusinessProfile entity.

primaryCategory.description: The primary category of the business on Google My Business.

additionalCategories.description: Additional categories associated with the business.

profileUrl.description: URL of the business's Google My Business profile.

Review.title: Review
Review.description: Represents a customer review for a business.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Review entity.

authorName.description: The name of the review author.

rating.description: The rating given by the customer (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

comment.description: The text content of the review.

date.description: The date the review was submitted.

isFiltered.description: Indicates whether the review was filtered internally (e.g., 1-4 stars).

internalFeedbackId.description: Reference to InternalFeedback. (Relationship: InternalFeedback 1:1 Review).  Only populated if review is filtered.

InternalFeedback.title: InternalFeedback
InternalFeedback.description: Represents internal feedback collected from customers who left a negative review.

id.description: Unique identifier for the InternalFeedback entity.

name.description: The name of the customer providing feedback.

email.description: The email address of the customer providing feedback.

message.description: The message provided by the customer.

AutomatedPost.title: AutomatedPost
AutomatedPost.description: Represents an automated post scheduled for a Google My Business profile.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AutomatedPost entity.

scheduledDate.description: The date and time the post is scheduled to be published.

status.description: The status of the post (e.g., scheduled, published, failed).

LandingPage.title: LandingPage
LandingPage.description: Represents a landing page created for a business.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LandingPage entity.

title.description: The title of the landing page.

content.description: The HTML or markdown content of the landing page.

design.description: JSON data representing the design and layout of the landing page.

url.description: The URL of the landing page.

Subscription.title: Subscription
Subscription.description: Represents a subscription plan for a business.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscription entity.

name.description: The name of the subscription plan (e.g., Basic, Premium).

price.description: The price of the subscription plan.

features.description: A list of features included in the subscription plan.

definition.description: Stores business information.  Root collection for all business-related data. Businesses can manage their Google My Business profiles, landing pages, etc.

params.name: businessId
params.description: The unique identifier for the business.

definition.description: Stores Google My Business profile information. Includes the businessId to ensure authorization independence for access control.

params.name: googleMyBusinessProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Google My Business profile.

definition.description: Stores customer reviews for a Google My Business profile.  Located as a subcollection to ensure efficient querying of reviews associated with a profile.  Includes fields for filtering (isFiltered) and internal feedback.

params.name: googleMyBusinessProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Google My Business profile.

params.name: reviewId
params.description: The unique identifier for the review.

definition.description: Stores internal feedback collected from customers who left negative reviews.  This is stored at the root to avoid authorization dependency on GoogleMyBusinessProfile.

params.name: internalFeedbackId
params.description: The unique identifier for the internal feedback.

definition.description: Stores automated posts scheduled for a Google My Business profile. Located as a subcollection to ensure efficient querying of posts associated with a profile.

params.name: googleMyBusinessProfileId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Google My Business profile.

params.name: automatedPostId
params.description: The unique identifier for the automated post.

definition.description: Stores landing pages created for a business. Located as a subcollection to ensure efficient querying of landing pages associated with a business.

params.name: businessId
params.description: The unique identifier for the business.

params.name: landingPageId
params.description: The unique identifier for the landing page.

params.name: subscriptionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the subscription.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore structure is designed to support the 'Local Leap' SaaS platform for Google Maps and Google My Business optimization. The structure prioritizes authorization independence, clarity, and scalability, aligning with the specified core design principles and strategy mandates.  Specifically, the structure ensures Authorization Independence by avoiding `get()` calls in rules. This is achieved via denormalization: for example, access to `GoogleMyBusinessProfile` and related subcollections (reviews, automated posts) is controlled by denormalizing the `businessId` from the `Business` entity. This ensures that security rules can validate access based on the `request.auth.uid` without needing to fetch data from parent documents. The structure is designed to easily facilitates the filtering of reviews into positive and negative flows. This is done by a simple query on a boolean filed within the `Review` entity. In addition, this structure supports the required QAPs. Private user data is stored under the `/users/{userId}` path, enabling path-based ownership. Data segregation is achieved by dedicating collections to distinct entities (e.g., `/businesses`, `/googleMyBusinessProfiles`, `/landingPages`).
